reasoning: The value on 2025-11-09 is bounded by the last published 2025-11-01 value of 2.704, which sits almost exactly at the median of the trailing 13 months. Although the series is highly volatile and historically exhibits some mean reversion, current macroeconomic fundamentals—including a slight uptick in headline CPI, multi-month highs in NY Fed consumer inflation expectations, and upward price pressures from newly implemented 2025 tariffs—suggest a positive drift for near-term inflation expectations. The linear trend model similarly points to an upward slope. I place the probability slightly above 50% for the first resolution date. In compliance with instructions, the probability for the more distant horizon is regressed closer to 0.5.
